user_stories.title: View Dashboard
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to view a dashboard that gives me an overview of all domains in the system so that I can navigate to the domains relevant to me.
business_value: Streamlines view domains for authorized users.

acceptance_criteria.description: User can see the domains they have access to on the dashboard.
Dashboard displays an empty state message when no domains are accessible to the user.
Each domain card displays: name, creation date, primary owner, and classification.
The dashboard shows three separate domain carousels: recently visited domains, primary owned domains, and draft domains.

Each domain card on the dashboard exposes a More Actions menu with context-sensitive options: owned domains additionally show "Grant access" and "Edit domain"; all cards show "View data feeds", "View statistics", "View questions", and "Survivorship".
Each domain card on the dashboard shows the count of pending access requests and open questions for that domain.
A "View all domains" button on the dashboard navigates the user to the full Domain Catalogue page.
The dashboard toolbar provides "Create Domain" and "Import domain" buttons that launch the respective flows without leaving the dashboard.

user_stories.title: Edit Data Feeds
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to edit data feeds so that I can keep data feed configuration up to date.
business_value: Streamlines data feed edit for authorized users.
preconditions: The user at least owns one data feed.

acceptance_criteria.description: User can edit fields such as owner tab, type of domain, target dataset, classification, Availability and schedule.
User cannot edit fields such domain name, type of domain and country code after creation of data feed.

user_stories.title: View Data Feed
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to view data feeds so that I can understand their configuration and status.
business_value: Streamlines view of data feeds for authorized users.
preconditions: The user has domain with data feed.

acceptance_criteria.description: User can access the data feeds details.
The user can see all data feeds across all domains, regardless of ownership.
Data feed detail view displays: name, description, availability status, and tabs for About, Data sources, Mapping, and Version management.

user_stories.title: Add Data Feeds
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to add data feeds so that I can make data sources available within a domain.
business_value: Streamlines data feeds addition for authorized users.
preconditions: User has at least one domain.
The user must be part of Dehdl-uat-za-trnd-sudo-users, Dehdl-uat-za-aqdt-sudo, UnifyAppAdmin-UAT-ZA-users, Pam-uat-za-dehftrnd-users, Pam-uat-za-dehftrnd-users.

acceptance_criteria.description: The user must capture all mandatory fields.
The user can import the data source from a file or from a Glue table when.

user_stories.title: Delete Data Feeds
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to delete data feeds so that I can remove data feeds that are no longer required.
business_value: Streamlines data feed deletion for authorized users.
preconditions: The user must be owner of domain.
The user must at least have one data feeds.
The user should own the data feed.

acceptance_criteria.description: User can delete data feeds.
User is prompted with a confirmation dialog before data feed deletion.
A user who does not own the data feed cannot delete it.

user_stories.title: Search Data Feeds
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to search among all data feeds so that I can quickly find the data feed I need.
business_value: Saves time for users managing numerous data feeds.
Improves navigation and clarity.
preconditions: User can search among all data feeds.

acceptance_criteria.description: User can search data feed by name or partial name.
Each data feed from search results displays its details such as data feed name, description, and the domain it belongs to.

user_stories.title: Domain Access - Direct Granting Access
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to view and direct access management from the domain page so that I can efficiently manage who has access to my domain.
business_value: Streamlines directly view and manage access domain for authorized users.
preconditions: The user must at least have one domain.

acceptance_criteria.description: The user can directly approve or reject the domain access request.
Only the domain owner can directly grant or reject access.
After approving or rejecting, the requester receives a status update notification.

user_stories.title: Request Access to Domain
user_stories.description: As a data consumer, I want to request access to a domain I do not own so that I can be granted the permissions needed to use its data.
business_value: Enables data consumers to gain access to domains they need.
Provides a governed, auditable access-request workflow.
preconditions: At least one domain exists that the user does not currently own or have access to.

acceptance_criteria.description: A user who is not the domain owner can open the Access tab on the Domain Detail page and see a "Request access" button.
After submitting an access request, the user receives an on-screen confirmation and the request appears in their Domain Access Rights page with status "Pending".
A user who has already submitted a pending request for a domain cannot submit a duplicate request for the same domain.
A domain owner cannot request access to their own domain; the "Request access" button is not shown for owned domains.

user_stories.title: Domain Access - Requests Approval
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to view bulk access request management from the Access Requests overview screen so that I can control who has access to my domain.
business_value: Streamlines access to domain access requests for domain owner.
preconditions: The user has at least one domain access request.

acceptance_criteria.description: User can view domain access request in states: Pending, Approved, and Rejected.
User can manage pending requests by approving or rejecting them.
Each item in the domain access request overview shows this information: domain name, domain version, requesting user, requested access level, and request timestamp.

user_stories.title: View My Domain Access Rights
user_stories.description: As a data consumer, I want to see all domain access requests I have submitted so that I can track the status of each request and act on any that need attention.
business_value: Gives data consumers full visibility into which domains they have requested or been granted access to.
preconditions: The user has submitted at least one domain access request.

acceptance_criteria.description: A user can open the "My access rights to domains" page from the Settings overlay and see a table listing all their access requests.
Each row in the table displays: domain name, domain version, requested access level, request status, and time of request.
A user can request access to a new domain directly from this page using the "Request access" button.
A user can view the detail of any access request in the list using the "View" action.
When the user has no access requests, the page displays an empty state.

user_stories.title: Domain Access - Overview of Users with Access
user_stories.description: As a domain owner, I want to see all users who have access to my domain, so that I can manage domain access effectively.
business_value: Improves navigation and clarity.
preconditions: At least one visible domain exists.
At least one user with access exists.

acceptance_criteria.description: User can search users with access by name or partial name.
Each users from search results shows this information: user name and Ab number.
User can search users among domains in primary of secondary ownership.

user_stories.title: Create Domain
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to create domain so that I can make it available for data consumers.
business_value: Streamlines domain creation for authorized users.

acceptance_criteria.description: User can create a new domain.
User can see an error message when entering information in the wrong format on cost center field.
Save button remains disabled until all mandatory fields are filled on create domain wizard {aspect} step. Aspect: About
The Create Domain wizard autosaves the user's progress; an autosave status indicator is visible and can be toggled on or off by the user.
The user can undo and redo changes made within the wizard before committing.

The wizard guides the user through sequential steps: About, Owner, Classification, Scheduling, Availability, and Target dataset; the user can continue to the next step or navigate back to a previous step.
In the Target dataset step, the user can add schema attributes manually, import them from a file, or populate them from a Glue table.

user_stories.title: View Domain Data Flow
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to view the data flow of a domain so that I can understand how data enters and exits the domain.
business_value: Gives users a visual map of how data moves through and around a domain, supporting impact analysis and audit readiness.

acceptance_criteria.description: A user can navigate to the "Data flow" tab on the Domain Detail page and the tab URL changes to /auth/all-domains/{domainId}/{version}/data-flow.
The Data flow tab displays a visualisation of the domain's upstream data sources and downstream consumers.
When a domain has no configured data sources or consumers, the tab shows an empty-state message.

user_stories.title: Delete Domain
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to delete a domain so that I can remove domains I no longer need.

user_stories.title: Edit Domain
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to edit domain details so that I can keep domain information accurate and up to date.
business_value: Streamlines domain edit for authorized users.
preconditions: The user owns at least one domain.

acceptance_criteria.description: The domain name and country are locked for edit after domain creation.
User can edit editable fields such as description, type of domain, target dataset, classification and schedule.
User can add target data set schema: manually, import from file, populate glue table.
Invalid updates on cost center shows an error message on label.
User can save changes — updated values are persisted and visible on reload.
User can cancel edit — original values are restored without saving.

user_stories.title: View Domain Hygiene (Survivorship)
user_stories.description: As a domain owner or steward, I want to view hygiene and survivorship metrics for a domain so that I can assess data quality, coverage, and consumer adoption at a glance.
business_value: Gives domain owners and stewards a quick health snapshot of their domain, covering data quality, coverage, consumer count, and optimisation score.

acceptance_criteria.description: A user can navigate to the Hygiene page for a domain via the "Survivorship" option in the domain card More Actions menu on the Dashboard.
The Hygiene page displays a metrics bar containing cards for: Coverage, Domain optimisation score, Total records from last run, Data quality score, and Consumers.
Each metric card displays the current computed value for the domain; when a metric is not yet available, the card shows an "under development" indicator.
The Hygiene page also displays the standard domain About information below the metrics bar, consistent with the Domain Detail About tab.

user_stories.title: Import Domain
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to import a domain from a JSON export file so that I can restore or replicate a domain without re-creating it manually.
business_value: Allows users to restore or share a domain by importing an exported JSON file.
Reduces manual re-creation effort.
preconditions: The user has a valid domain export JSON file.

acceptance_criteria.description: An authenticated user can open the Import Domain dialog from the Dashboard or from the All Domains page using the "Import domain" button.
The Import Domain dialog requires the user to provide a domain name and select a JSON file before the submit button becomes enabled.
After a successful import the dialog closes and the newly imported domain is visible in the domain list.
A user who submits an invalid or malformed JSON file is shown an error message and the dialog remains open for correction.
A user can cancel the import at any point; no domain is created and the dialog closes.

user_stories.title: Search Domain
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to search among all domains so that I can quickly find the domain I need.
business_value: Saves time for users managing numerous domains.
Improves navigation and clarity.
preconditions: At least one visible domain exist.

acceptance_criteria.description: User can search domain by name or partial name.
Each domain from search results displays its domain details card such as name, version, primary owner, creation date, and domain type.
A user can filter the domain list by one or more status values: Active, Draft, Cold, and Testing; the list updates to show only domains matching the selected statuses.
When the search term and applied filters produce no matching domains, the list displays an empty-state message.

user_stories.title: Manage Domain Versions
user_stories.description: As a domain owner, I want to manage the lifecycle versions of my domain so that I can evolve domain schema and configuration without disrupting active consumers.
business_value: Enables domain owners to evolve a domain through a structured lifecycle (Draft → Testing → Active → Cold) without disrupting current consumers.
preconditions: The user is the primary owner of the domain.

acceptance_criteria.description: A domain owner can navigate to the "Version management" tab on the Domain Detail page and see the current version and its lifecycle status.
A domain owner can create a new draft version of a domain from the Version management tab; the new version appears with status "Draft".
A domain owner can start the testing phase for a draft version; the version status changes to "Testing".
A domain owner can make an active version cold from the Version management tab; the version status changes to "Cold".
A domain owner can delete a version from the Version management tab after confirming a deletion prompt; the version no longer appears in the list.
A user who is not the domain owner sees the Version management tab in read-only mode — lifecycle action buttons are not visible.

user_stories.title: View Domain
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to view the details of a selected domain so that I can understand its details and manage it effectively.
business_value: Streamlines domain details visibility for authorized users.
preconditions: User can see at least one domain.

acceptance_criteria.description: User can access the domain details, schema, run history, data feed, data flow, statistics, question, access and version management.
Domain card details on dashboard displays information about name, creation date, primary owner attributes summary details about access request, questions and classification.
Each Domain Detail tab (About, Schema, Run history, Data feed, Data flow, Statistics, Questions, Access, Version management) has its own distinct URL, allowing deep-linking directly to any tab.
The Schema tab displays the domain attribute list in a tree table; when no attributes are defined, an empty state is shown.

The More Actions menu on the Domain Detail page shows a different set of actions depending on ownership: domain owners see "Edit domain", "Delete domain", "Grant access", and "Manage versions" in addition to the options available to all users.

user_stories.title: On-screen Notifications
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to receive on-screen notifications so that I can stay informed about changes in real time.
business_value: User wants to stay informed about changes.
Streamlines notifications for authorized users.
preconditions: User has necessary permissions to receive notifications.

acceptance_criteria.description: An on-screen notification is displayed when a domain is successfully created.
An on-screen notification is displayed when a question is successfully created.
An on-screen notification is displayed when domain is successfully modified.
An on-screen notification is displayed when access to domain successfully requested.
An on-screen notification is displayed when user successfully replied on a question.
An on-screen notification is displayed when question is marked as helpful.
An on-screen notification is displayed when question is unmarked as helpful.
An on-screen notification is displayed when data feed successfully added.
An on-screen notification is displayed when data feed edited successfully.
An on-screen notification is displayed when a question is successfully deleted.
An on-screen notification is displayed when a domain is successfully deleted.
An on-screen notification is displayed when a data feed is successfully deleted.
An on-screen notification is displayed when a domain access request is approved or rejected.
An on-screen notification is displayed when a domain is successfully imported.

user_stories.title: Close Question
user_stories.description: As a question owner, I want to close one or more questions so that I can mark them as resolved and prevent further replies.
business_value: Streamlines of closing question for users.
preconditions: Only the owner of question can close a question.
User can close to any question in state OPEN.
User has at least one question exists.

acceptance_criteria.description: Only the owner can close one or more questions.
A closed question cannot receive new replies.
User cannot close an already-closed question.

user_stories.title: Create Question
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to ask a question so that I can get help or information from other users.
business_value: Streamlines question creation for authorized users.

acceptance_criteria.description: The user clicks on tab question to access ask a question button to fill in all mandatory fields such as subject, description and select a domain for a new question.
The post question button is enabled when all mandatory fields are filled.
Question can hold any amount of Tags including no one.
A successfully submitted question is immediately visible in the question list.

user_stories.title: Delete Question
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to delete a question so that I can remove questions that are no longer relevant.
business_value: Streamlines question deletion for authorized users.
preconditions: The user must be owner of question.

acceptance_criteria.description: User can delete question.
User is prompted with a confirmation dialog before question deletion.
A user who is not the question owner cannot delete the question.

user_stories.title: Mark Question Helpful
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to mark replies to questions as helpful so that the community can identify the best answer.
business_value: Streamlines marking of question replies for authorized users.
preconditions: User has a reply to their question.

acceptance_criteria.description: The question owner can mark one reply on their question as helpful.
A question can have at most one reply marked as helpful at a time.

user_stories.title: Reply Question
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to reply to a question so that I can contribute answers to other users' questions.
business_value: Streamlines question reply for users.
preconditions: User can reply to any question in state OPEN.

acceptance_criteria.description: User can reply one or more questions.
Each question can have more than one dialogue reply.

user_stories.title: Search Question
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to search among all questions so that I can quickly find questions relevant to me.
business_value: Saves time for users managing numerous questions.
Improves navigation and clarity.
preconditions: User can search among all questions.

acceptance_criteria.description: User can search question by name or partial name.
Each question from search results displays its details such as subject, question details, domain name, creation date, and question owner.

user_stories.title: Unmark Helpful Question Reply
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to unmark replies on questions that were marked as helpful so that I can correct my helpful marking when needed.
business_value: Streamlines unmark replies on question that marked as helpful.
preconditions: User has a reply on their question that is marked as helpful.

acceptance_criteria.description: User can unmark one or more questions that marked as helpful.
After unmarking, the reply no longer displays the helpful indicator.
Only the question owner can unmark a helpful reply.

user_stories.title: View Question
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to view question details so that I can read its full context and responses.
business_value: Streamlines view question details for authorized users.
preconditions: The user can see all questions asked by other users.

acceptance_criteria.description: User can access the question details.
Question details displays information such as subject, details, domain, creation date and the question owner.

user_stories.title: User Login
user_stories.description: As a user with valid credentials, I want to log into the Unify application so that I can securely access the application.
business_value: Ensures secure, controlled access
Prevents unauthorized access to application
preconditions: The user is part of the PAM/dehdl group and has valid credentials.

acceptance_criteria.description: The login screen is accessible and displays all {aspects}. Aspects: username field, password field, login button
The user with valid username and password successfully logs in.
The user with invalid username and password is prompted with error message on screen.
An authenticated user whose session has expired is redirected to the login screen with an informative message.
A user who is already logged in and navigates to the login URL is automatically redirected to the dashboard.
The login button should be disabled until {aspects} fields are filled out. Aspects: username field, password field
A user can initiate login via the MSAL SSO option on the login screen and is redirected through the corporate identity provider to complete authentication.

user_stories.title: User Logout
user_stories.description: As a user, I want to log out of the application so that I can end my session securely.
business_value: Ensures secure, controlled access
Consistent user experience across application

acceptance_criteria.description: The user can log out and is redirected to the login screen.
Submitting valid credentials navigates to the dashboard.
A user with an active session navigating to the login page is automatically redirected to the dashboard without seeing the login form.

functionalities.title: Authentication Screen — Login Form Initial State

acceptance_criteria.description: On load the form renders: logo, username field, password field, and login button.
The login button is disabled while either the username or password field is empty.
Submitting invalid credentials displays an error notification on screen.
On load of any authenticated page, the toolbar renders the global search control, the settings control, and the user menu trigger.
The user menu trigger displays the signed-in user's display name and role.
Selecting the Dashboard or Domains sidebar item routes to that page.
Selecting the global search control routes to the domain catalogue.
For the standard user role, selecting the Domain Access or Community sidebar items does not navigate away from the current page.
On load the dashboard displays the primary-owned domains carousel.
Domains where the user is a secondary steward appear in the secondary-steward domains carousel.
Draft domains appear in the draft-domains carousel.
Selecting Create Domain routes to the create-domain wizard About step.
Selecting Import domain opens the import domain dialog.

functionalities.title: Dashboard — Domain Card Actions by Ownership

acceptance_criteria.description: For a non-owned domain, the card actions menu offers view-only actions (view data feeds, view statistics, view questions, survivorship) and disables Edit domain.
For an owned domain, the card actions menu additionally offers Grant access and an enabled Edit domain action.
The catalogue presents a "Search domains" field with a GO control that searches the domain list for the entered term.
The filter panel offers domain status filters (Active, Cold, Draft, Testing) that are applied to the list via Apply filters.
Selecting Clear filter removes the applied filters.

functionalities.title: Domain Catalogue — Navigate to Domain Detail

acceptance_criteria.description: Selecting a domain name in the catalogue routes to that domain's About detail page for its version.
On load the About tab displays the domain name, domain type, primary owner, and creation date.
The About tab displays the adoption status, cost centre, cost centre owner, and tenant id.
Selecting a domain detail tab routes to that tab's dedicated URL for the domain version (About, Schema, Run history, Data feed, Data flow, Statistics, Questions, Access, Version management).
For an owned domain, the More actions menu offers View data feeds, View statistics, Grant access, Edit domain, Delete domain, Manage versions, and Export domain.
For a non-owned domain, the More actions menu offers View data feeds, View statistics, Manage versions, and Export domain only (no Grant access, Edit domain, or Delete domain).

functionalities.title: Create Domain Wizard — About Field Validation

acceptance_criteria.description: The cost centre field accepts a value of two letters followed by six digits and rejects any other format.
Submitting a domain name that already exists is rejected as a duplicate.
The cost centre owner is chosen via an autocomplete that matches a partial term and resolves to the full owner option.
Selecting Continue advances the wizard through its steps in order: About, Owner, Classification, Scheduling, Availability, Target dataset.
Selecting Cancel exits the create-domain wizard.

functionalities.title: Create Domain Wizard — Draft Editing Controls

acceptance_criteria.description: The wizard toolbar presents an autosave toggle and an autosave status indicator reflecting the save state.
The wizard toolbar presents undo, redo, and commit controls for the draft domain.
The final step displays read-only summary sections for About, Ownership, Classification, Availability, Scheduling, and Schema, each with an Edit control that returns to the corresponding step.
A success notification is displayed with a link to the created domain.
In edit mode the domain name field is disabled and cannot be changed.
In edit mode the domain type is disabled and cannot be changed.
In edit mode the description, cost centre, and adoption status remain editable.
Editing an editable field in edit mode (description, cost centre, or adoption status) persists the change via autosave.
A search field with a GO control is presented to search the domain's questions list.
A sort selector and a filter panel with an Apply control are presented to reorder and filter the questions list.
Selecting Ask a question routes to the ask-question form.
The ask-question form presents a subject field, a details editor, a tag entry, and a post control.
Selecting a question in the list routes to that question's detail page.

functionalities.title: Data Feed Populate Wizard — Step Progression

acceptance_criteria.description: Selecting Continue advances the populate wizard through its stepper.
Selecting Cancel exits the populate wizard, and selecting Save persists the in-progress data feed.
Selecting an access status (Pending, Approved, or Rejected) filters the requests list to that status.
Enabling "show only domains in my primary ownership" restricts the list to domains the user primarily owns.
The access rights table lists the user's requests with columns Domain, Domain version, Requested access, Status, and Time of request.
A Request access control and a per-row View control are presented.
On load the data feed detail page displays the feed name, description, and availability.
The page presents the About, Data sources, Mapping, and Version management tabs.
Selecting the Data flow tab routes to the domain's data-flow page for its version.
Selecting Survivorship from a domain card's action menu opens the domain hygiene view.
The hygiene view displays metric cards for Coverage, Domain optimisation score, Total records from last run, Data quality score, and Consumers.
The import dialog presents a domain name field, a choose-file control, and submit and cancel controls.
Choosing a file replaces the default "No file selected" text with the selected file name.
Submission requires a valid .json domain export file to be attached.

features.title: Add Schema Attribute Dialog
features.route: modal overlay — no dedicated URL; triggered from the Target Dataset step of Create Domain (FEAT-006) and Edit Domain (FEAT-007) wizards
features.purpose: Modal form for adding/editing schema attributes (name, description, type, nullable).

features.title: Application Shell
features.purpose: Persistent authenticated shell present on every /auth/* page; provides left-sidebar navigation and top toolbar.

features.title: Domain Questions  [cross-reference]
features.purpose: Full-page form for submitting a question on a domain.

features.title: Create Domain Wizard  [cross-reference]
features.route: /auth/all-domains/create-domain/about  (stays on this URL for all steps)
features.purpose: Step 1 (About) — domain name, description, type, adoption status, cost centre, and tenant ID fields.

features.title: Create Domain Wizard  [cross-reference]
features.purpose: Shared wizard toolbar (autosave, undo/redo, cancel, continue, submit) and step-header navigation helper.

features.title: Dashboard
features.purpose: Home screen for authenticated users; surfaces owned-domain, draft-domain, and recently-visited-domain carousels.

features.title: Data Feed Detail
features.purpose: Detail view for a specific data feed version; shows About, Data sources, Mapping, and Version management tabs.

features.title: Data Feed Populate Wizard
features.purpose: Multi-step wizard for populating a data feed on a domain.

features.title: Domain Data Flow
features.purpose: Data Flow tab on the domain detail page — visualises data lineage / flow for a specific domain version.

features.title: Domain Access Control
features.purpose: Settings page for domain owners to review and action access requests (Requests approval · Direct granting tabs).

features.title: Domain Access Rights
features.purpose: Settings page showing the authenticated user's own access request history across all domains.

features.title: Domain Detail
features.purpose: Domain detail page with nine sub-tabs: About, Schema, Run history, Data feed, Data flow, Statistics, Questions, Access, Version management.

features.title: Domain Questions
features.purpose: Page for viewing, searching, filtering, and posting questions scoped to a specific domain version.

features.title: Domain Catalogue
features.purpose: Searchable and filterable catalogue listing all domains available to the authenticated user.

features.title: Edit Domain Wizard  [cross-reference]
features.purpose: Step 1 (About) — domain description, type, adoption status, cost centre, and tenant ID fields. domain-name is disabled in edit mode.

features.title: Edit Domain Wizard  [cross-reference]
features.purpose: Step 5 (Availability) — domain availability frequency and schedule.

features.title: Edit Domain Wizard  [cross-reference]
features.purpose: Step 3 (Classification) — domain classification tags management.

features.title: Edit Domain Wizard  [cross-reference]
features.purpose: Step 2 (Owner) — domain owners/stewards table management.

features.title: Edit Domain Wizard
features.purpose: Multi-step wizard for editing an existing domain definition.

features.title: Edit Domain Wizard  [cross-reference]
features.purpose: Step 4 (Scheduling) — domain scheduling and runtime parameters.

features.title: Create Domain Wizard  [cross-reference]
features.purpose: Final summary page shown after wizard completion (Create or Edit). Displays success notification and read-only section summaries with Edit buttons for navigating back to specific steps.

features.title: Edit Domain Wizard  [cross-reference]
features.purpose: Step 6 (Target dataset) — schema attributes table and import schema actions.

features.title: Edit Domain Wizard  [cross-reference]
features.purpose: Shared wizard toolbar (autosave, undo/redo, cancel, continue, submit) and step-header navigation helper.

features.title: Domain Hygiene
features.purpose: Hygiene view for a domain version — shows a metrics bar (Coverage, Domain optimisation score, Total records from last run, Data quality score, Consumers) alongside the domain detail content.

features.title: Import Domain Dialog
features.route: modal overlay — no dedicated URL; triggered from Dashboard (FEAT-003) and Domain Catalogue (FEAT-004)
features.purpose: Modal overlay for importing an existing domain definition from a file; accessible from Dashboard and Domain Catalogue.

features.title: Authentication Screen
features.purpose: Authentication screen where users enter credentials to gain access to the application.
